Question: Which elements were discovered by Marie Curie?
Expected Answer:
Marie Curie discovered two elements: 1. Polonium (Po)
2. Radium (Ra)
Actual Answer:
Marie Curie, along with her husband Pierre Curie, was a pioneering physicist and chemist. Together, they discovered the elements 
polonium (Po) and radium (Ra). They are the only married couple to have won a Nobel Prize, with Marie Curie winning the Nobel Prize 
in Physics in 1903 and Pierre Curie winning the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1903 as well, and later Marie Curie winning the Nobel 
Prize in Chemistry in 1911.

It's worth noting that Marie Curie also played a key role in the discovery of another element, thorium (Th), but the discovery was 
not solely hers.
